info.title: LegalDispatch Service of Process API
info.description:
Service of process business logic, compliance, and proof generation for the
LegalDispatch platform. Manages order lifecycle, fee line items, party tracking,
partner transfers, and proof of service generation.

## Authentication Model
- **Public endpoints** â€” `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` (JWT from API Gateway)

## Response Format
All responses use **snake_case** JSON serialization. Error responses follow the
standard envelope: `{ "code": "CODE", "message": "...", "details": {...}, "request_id": "..." }`.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of service requests for the authenticated user's partner.
Supports filtering by **status**, free-text **search** (order number, case number),
sorting, and pagination.

### Query Parameters
- `status` â€” filter by status (e.g., `draft`, `submitted`, `in_progress`)
- `search` â€” free-text search across order number and case number
- `sort_by` â€” column to sort by (default: `created_at`)
- `sort_direction` â€” `asc` or `desc` (default: `desc`)
- `page` â€” page number, 1-based (default: `1`)
- `page_size` â€” items per page, 1â€“100 (default: `25`)

post.description:
Creates a new service request in **draft** status. The request may include
parties and locations inline. Fee calculation is performed immediately via PMS.

Set `client_id` to create an order on behalf of a client.

get.description:
Returns a single service request with full details including parties,
locations, fee line items, and attempt summaries.

post.description:
Submits a **draft** service request. This triggers:
1. Completeness validation (parties, locations, documents)
2. Payment hold placement via Payment Service
3. Work order creation in Operations Service
4. Status transition to `submitted` (or `fee_review` if partner requires)

The `payment_method_id` identifies the payment source for the hold.

Cancels a service request. Only allowed for orders in a pre-`in_progress` status
(`draft`, `fee_review`, `submitted`, `assigned`).

Cancellation releases any payment hold and cancels the Operations work order if one exists.

Initiates a transfer of the service request to another partner.
The target partner's acceptance is checked via PMS before the transfer is created.

Only allowed for orders in `submitted` status. The order transitions to `pending_transfer`
until the target partner accepts or rejects.

The receiving partner accepts or rejects a pending transfer.

- **Accept**: Order ownership transfers, fees are recalculated, and the work order
  is re-created under the new partner.
- **Reject**: The transfer is cancelled, the order reverts to `submitted` status
  under the original partner, and a refund is issued.

The `decision` field must be `accept` or `reject`. When rejecting,
`rejection_reason` should be provided.

Adds an additional fee to a service request that is in `submitted`, `assigned`,
`dispatched`, or `in_progress` status.

If `charge_immediately` is `true`, the fee is charged via Payment Service immediately.
Otherwise, the fee is tracked and charged upon order completion.

Fee types: `mileage`, `wait_time`, `rush`, `after_hours`, `stakeout`, `skip_trace`, `other`.

Approves or adjusts the fee line items for a service request in `fee_review` status.

### Actions
- `approve` â€” accept all fees as-is and proceed to submission
- `adjust` â€” modify specific fee line item amounts before approval

When adjusting, provide an `adjustments` array with fee line item IDs and new amounts.
The estimated fee is recalculated from the adjusted line items.
After approval, the order transitions to `submitted`.

Requests assignment of a process server to the service request.
Delegates to Operations Service, which will send a callback when the assignment is confirmed.

Only allowed for orders in `submitted` status.

Requests dispatch of the work order to the assigned process server.
Delegates to Operations Service, which will send a callback when dispatch is confirmed.

Optional `dispatch_method`: `email`, `app`, `sms` (default: `app`).
Only allowed for orders in `assigned` status.

post.summary: Bulk assign server to batch orders
post.description:
Assigns a process server to multiple service requests from a batch import.
Each order is assigned individually â€” partial success is possible.

Returns the count of successful assignments and any errors per order.

put.description:
Updates the internal operations notes on a service request.
These notes are visible only to operations staff and are not included in client-facing views.

post.summary: Add a service location to a party
post.description:
Adds a new service location to an existing party within the service request.
The location is added with the specified priority and marked as active.

This endpoint is used when operations discovers an additional address for a party
after the order has been created.

post.summary: Batch create service requests from CSV
post.description:
Accepts a parsed CSV batch import and creates one service request per valid row.
Invalid rows are tracked as errors on the batch record.

The request body contains the file metadata and parsed row data. The actual CSV file
should be uploaded to Document Management first, and the `file_id` provided here.

Returns the batch import record with progress statistics.

get.description:
Returns the current status and progress of a batch import,
including total rows, successful imports, failed imports, and error details.

rejection_reason.description: Required when string AcceptTransferRequest.Decision is `reject`. Reason for rejection.

charge_immediately.description: Whether to charge the fee immediately via Payment Service.

is_charged.description: Whether the fee has been charged.

additional_fees_total.description: Updated additional fees total on the service request.

party_id.description: Party ID to add the location to.

AddLocationRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/service-requests/:id/locations`.
Adds a location to a party on an existing service request.

server_id.description: ID of the process server to assign.

AssignServerRequest.description: Request body for `POST /{id}/assign`. Identifies the process server to assign.

BatchImportFileRequest.description: Metadata about a batch import file, validated before processing begins.

BatchImportResponse.description: Response shape for a batch import record.

server_id.description: ID of the process server to assign to all specified orders.

BulkAssignRequest.description: Request body for `POST /batch/{batchId}/assign`. Bulk assignment payload.

CancelServiceRequestRequest.description: Request body for cancelling a service request.

client_id.description: Optional client ID for on-behalf-of creation.

parties.description: Parties to serve from the filing.

CreateFromFilingRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /internal/create-from-filing`.
Sent by Filing Service to auto-create a service request.

priority.description: Priority order for this location (1 = highest). Defaults to 1.

contact_phone.description: Optional phone number for the party.

contact_email.description: Optional email address for the party.

locations.description: Service locations for this party. At least one required for submission.

court_id.description: Court identifier where the case is filed.

service_type.description: Type of service â€” currently only `personal`.

hearing_date.description: Related court hearing date and time.

hearing_location.description: Courtroom or location for the hearing.

client_id.description:
Optional client ID for on-behalf-of orders. When set, the order is created
on behalf of this client by a partner operations user.

payment_method_id.description: Optional payment method ID. Required at submission, optional at draft creation.

parties.description: Parties to serve, each with locations. At least one required for submission.

CreateServiceRequestRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/service-requests`.
Creates a new service request in `draft` status.

DispatchOrderRequest.description: Request body for `POST /{id}/dispatch`. Specifies dispatch configuration.

fee_line_item_id.description: ID of the fee line item to adjust.

new_amount.description: New amount for the fee line item.

FeeAdjustment.description: Individual fee line item adjustment during fee review.

action.description: Approval action: `approve` (no changes) or `adjust` (with line item modifications).

adjustments.description:
Adjusted line items. Required when string FeeApprovalRequest.Action is `adjust`.
Only modified items need to be included; omitted items retain their original amount.

FeeApprovalRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/service-requests/:id/approve-fees`.
Approves or adjusts fee line items during fee review.

estimated_fee.description: Updated estimated fee total after approval/adjustment.

adjusted_count.description: Number of line items that were adjusted.

FilingLocation.description: Location data from a filing service request.

name.description: Name of the party to be served.

FilingParty.description: Party data from a filing service request.

to_partner_id.description: Target partner ID to transfer the order to.

transfer_notes.description: Optional notes explaining the reason for the transfer.

InitiateTransferRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/service-requests/:id/transfer`.
Initiates a transfer to another partner.

PaginatedResponseOfServiceRequestRespons.description: Paginated list response for service requests.

created_on_behalf.description: Whether the order was created on behalf of a client.

updated_at.description: When the order was last updated.

ServiceRequestResponse.description: Full response shape for a service request, including nested parties and fees.

payment_method_id.description: Payment method ID to place the hold against.

tags.name: Service Requests
tags.description: Order creation, listing, detail retrieval, and lifecycle operations

tags.name: Submission
tags.description: Order submission and fee review workflow

tags.name: Batch
tags.description: CSV batch import and status tracking
